idea-plugin.name: MockServer
idea-plugin.description:
MockServer lets you easily mock any system you integrate with via HTTP or HTTPS (services, REST or RPC APIs, and more), so you can develop and test against simulated dependencies instead of real ones. This plugin brings MockServer controls into IntelliJ IDEA and all other JetBrains IDEs, so you can run and inspect a local mock server without leaving the editor.

Features

  • Expectation file validation — name a file *.mockserver.json (or *.mockserver.jsonc) and the IDE validates it as you type, with autocompletion and hover documentation, using the same schema MockServer validates against. A single expectation or an array of expectations (initialization JSON) is accepted.
  • Start MockServer (Docker) — launches the official mockserver/mockserver container (default port 1080) with a single click.
  • Open MockServer Dashboard — opens the live dashboard in your browser to watch requests as they arrive, inspect why a request did or didn't match, and review active expectations.
  • Open MockServer Dashboard in IDE — embeds the live dashboard inside the IDE in a dedicated tool window using the bundled JCEF (Chromium) engine, with Reload and Open-in-Browser controls. Falls back to the external browser when JCEF is unavailable.
  • Reset MockServer — clears all expectations and recorded logs on the running server with a single confirmed click.
  • MockServer tool window — a dockable panel in the bottom tool-window bar that surfaces every action as a one-click button, grouped into server controls and editor actions, so the full action set is always within reach without opening the Tools menu.
  • Settings — configure the Docker image, container name, and port under Settings | Tools | MockServer.

Usage

Run the actions from the Tools > MockServer menu or the MockServer tool window. A typical flow: author a *.mockserver.json file with validation and completion, Start MockServer (Docker), create expectations by sending JSON to /mockserver/expectation (or via any MockServer client library), then Open MockServer Dashboard to inspect traffic.

Requirements

  • Docker must be installed and running to use the Start MockServer (Docker) action. The dashboard action only needs a running MockServer (started by this plugin or anywhere else) reachable at localhost:1080.

Full documentation — creating expectations, verifying requests, the REST API, and client libraries for Java, JavaScript, Python, Ruby, Go, .NET, Rust and PHP — is at www.mock-server.com.
idea-plugin.change-notes: • 7.1.0 — Start MockServer via Docker, open the MockServer Dashboard, and a dedicated MockServer tool window, all from within the IDE.

action.description: Opens the MockServer dashboard in your default browser
Shows the live MockServer dashboard inside the IDE using the bundled Chromium engine
Starts a MockServer container via Docker
Sends the active editor file to PUT /mockserver/expectation on the running MockServer
Retrieves expectations recorded from proxied traffic and opens them in a new JSON tab
Sends the active editor's OpenAPI/Swagger spec to MockServer and opens the generated expectations
Sends the active editor's ad-hoc HTTP request to the running MockServer and opens the response
Fetches MockServer's mock-drift records and opens a readable text report in a new tab
Finds every received request belonging to a W3C trace id (or traceparent) and opens them in a new JSON tab
Uploads a compiled .wasm custom-rule module to the running MockServer (PUT /mockserver/wasm/modules)
Lists the WASM custom-rule modules registered on the running MockServer (GET /mockserver/wasm/modules)
Clears all expectations and logs on the running MockServer (PUT /mockserver/reset)
